selection_rule: Best mean OOS score across pure holdout + walk-forward folds. Must beat prior OOS champ v34_bag6_opts.

champion_oos.fold_wins: 4/5 vs v29 AND 4/5 vs v34_bag6; loses only wf_fold3 (2026H1)

interaction_finding: bag8 WITHOUT soft-structure overlay FAILS OOS (-0.039); overlay's weak-structure downsizing is what makes the wide bag safe. Composition > either component.

growth_sleeve_not_default.note: 70/30 with v32 soft_strong, monthly rebal, 4/5 fold wins. soft_strong alone still FAILS OOS - never unblended.

killed_this_loop: v32 soft_struct/strong standalone: OOS fail (-0.0017 / 0.0089) — +108-113% full-window is path-dependent
risk_pct 15-20%: less growth (equity budget depletion) AND OOS collapse (r20 mean -0.0285)
vol_z boost sizing (0.0195) and fractional-Kelly sizing (0.0169): both below v29 baseline
v35_bag8 (wide bag, no overlay): OOS -0.039 - wide bag unsafe without soft-structure sizing
caveats: wf_fold3 (2026H1) is v35's one lost fold (+4.9% vs v34 +13.9%, DD -15%); v34_bag6 is the fallback if the newest regime persists.
The two big 2025 OOS folds overlap in calendar (~1 independent observation, not 2); wins spread across TSLA/RKLB/IONQ/GME.
~20 variants scored on the same 5 folds this cycle - fold selection pressure is real; treat live paper fills as true holdout.
1k->1M at full-window CAGR ~24.5y (default) / ~21.7y (sleeve); no honest config reaches 1000x in single-digit years.
